name: Argo Mine & Mill
close_reason: WW2 War Production Board L-208 order shut down all non-essential gold mining in 1942. Argo never restarted at full capacity; the Argo Tunnel collapse in 1943 effectively ended operations.
claim_status: Privately owned, operational as historic tourist mine (Argo Gold Mine & Mill)
recovery_method_used: Mercury amalgamation + stamp mill + cyanide leach (after 1900)
modern_recovery_method: Gravity concentration + flotation + cyanide vat leach
satellite_signature: Strong gold-bearing sulfide signature visible on Sentinel-2 ratios B11/B12 + B4/B2
geological_formation: Idaho Springs Formation - Precambrian gneiss + Cenozoic intrusive porphyries
tailings_volume_estimate: 3.8M tons accessible tailings within 500m of portal

name: Phoenix Gold Mine
close_reason: Continuously held; small-scale operation today as working tour mine. Full-scale reactivation gated on permit + capital.
claim_status: Privately held by Phoenix Mine LLC
recovery_method_used: Stamp mill + amalgamation early, modern gravity later
satellite_signature: Iron oxide alteration halo confirmed Sentinel-2 B11/B8a
geological_formation: Idaho Springs Formation - quartz vein system in gneiss

name: Lamartine Mine
close_reason: Silver price collapse 1893 plus exhaustion of high-grade ore at accessible depths. Some intermittent leasing through 1935, then full abandonment.
claim_status: BLM-administered, claim status currently open in MLRS lookup
satellite_signature: Argillic alteration band Sentinel-2 B6/B7 ratio
geological_formation: Idaho Springs Formation - polymetallic vein system

name: Bobtail Mine
production_volume: $3M+ in gold (historical dollars) - one of the original Pikes Peak Gold Rush mines
close_reason: Original 1859 discovery mine. Sustained operations through 1920s; closed due to flooding at depth (water table rise after Argo Tunnel drainage shifted regional hydrology) and rising operational costs vs. dropping ore grade.
claim_status: Mixed - some patented claims privately held, BLM administering surrounding land
recovery_method_used: Mercury amalgamation + arrastra + later stamp mill
modern_recovery_method: Tailings re-treatment with gravity + cyanide + mercury sequestration (QMSS integration recommended)
satellite_signature: Heavy iron oxide + sulfide weathering signature
geological_formation: Central City District - Precambrian gneiss + Tertiary intrusives
tailings_volume_estimate: 2.4M tons (with adjacent Glory Hole + Gregory tailings)

name: Glory Hole Mine (Patch)
production_volume: Estimated 200,000+ oz Au cumulative across all phases
close_reason: Open-pit operations from a series of stoped-out underground workings caved-in over decades. Final closure 1937 due to ore exhaustion at then-economics; the deep ore body remains.
claim_status: Partially heritage-protected, surrounding BLM land has open claim opportunities
recovery_method_used: Surface stoping + mercury amalgamation + stamp mill
satellite_signature: Tellurium-anomaly cluster (rare) - Sentinel-2 B12 + ASTER thermal anomaly
geological_formation: Central City District - calaverite-bearing telluride vein
critical_minerals_bycatch: Tellurium (Central City District is a known telluride province)

name: Caribou Mine
production_volume: $30M+ silver (historical dollars) - one of Colorado's most productive silver mines
close_reason: Silver price collapse of 1893 (Sherman Silver Purchase Act repeal) cratered profitability. Intermittent operations through 1947, then full closure due to flooding and depleted high-grade ore at accessible depths.
claim_status: Heritage area + adjacent BLM open claims
listing_price: $48,000 (adjacent open BLM claims, not the heritage Caribou itself)
satellite_signature: Strong argillic + sericite alteration Sentinel-2 B6/B7

name: Cardinal Mine
production_volume: Significant WW1+WW2 tungsten producer for US war effort
close_reason: Tungsten price collapsed post-Korean-War as global supply restored. Operation ceased 1958. Critical-minerals interest renewed circa 2024 (lithium spodumene noted in historic core).
claim_status: Mixed private + open BLM claims adjacent
modern_recovery_method: Modern flotation + spodumene leach for Li
geological_formation: Boulder Tungsten District - Precambrian schist host with pegmatite intrusions

name: Comet Mine
close_reason: Owner death 1928; estate dissolution; ore body never independently re-evaluated. Remains underexplored.
claim_status: Open BLM claim - currently no active claimant in MLRS
satellite_signature: Iron oxide alteration weak but present

name: Five-Twenty Mine
production_volume: Modest producer in Gilpin County boom
close_reason: Silver Panic of 1893 cascading effects + accessible-vein exhaustion under 19th century recovery tech (50-55% gold capture at the time).
claim_status: Private estate held, listed for sale
modern_recovery_method: Tailings re-treatment with QMSS integration + cyanide leach
satellite_signature: Strong sulfide weathering + iron oxide halo

name: Russell Gulch Workings
close_reason: Patchwork of small operations; mostly abandoned after 1893 silver crash, with last activity through 1916 at lower grades. The waste piles are the asset today.
claim_status: Multiple BLM open claims + heritage waste piles surveyed by State Geological Survey 2019
geological_formation: Central City District - polymetallic vein cluster

name: Barney-Mike-Stevens Group
close_reason: Sustained silver production until 1893 collapse; intermittent lessee operations through 1925 at high cost. Closed due to operational economics, not ore exhaustion.
claim_status: Open BLM claim - MLRS shows no current claimant
recovery_method_used: Stamp mill + selective flotation late period

name: Alps Mine
production_volume: Documented production approximately 38,000 oz Au across operational period
close_reason: Cave-in during 1907 collapse killed two miners. Owner's family closed operations rather than rebuild. Ore body never independently re-evaluated.
claim_status: Open BLM claim - currently no active claimant
geological_formation: Central City District - gold-bearing quartz veins
